2605.18672.title: Position: A Three-Layer Probabilistic Assume-Guarantee Architecture Is Structurally Required for Safe LLM Agent Deployment
2605.18672.paper_summary: This position paper argues that enforcing LLM agent safety within a single abstraction layer is not merely suboptimal but categorically insufficient for deployed LLM agents -- a structural consequence of how agent execution works, not a contingent limitation of current systems. The three dimensions that jointly constitute safe operation -- semantic intent and policy compliance, environmental validity, and dynamical feasibility -- each depend on a strictly distinct set of information that becomes available at different stages of execution. No single guardrail can certify all three. We argue that the community must respond with a contract-based architecture in which each safety dimension is enforced by an independently certified layer whose probabilistic guarantee satisfies the next layer's assumption. We sketch such an architecture and derive the compositional system-level safety bounds it admits via the chain rule of probability. Three open problems stand between this and a deployable standard: bound estimation from non-i.i.d.\ traces, graceful degradation of contracts under deployment drift, and extension to multi-agent settings -- the most important unfinished business in LLM agent runtime assurance.
2605.18672.paper_summary_zh: 這份立場文件主張，在單一抽象層內強制執行LLM代理的安全性不僅是次優的，更是對已部署的LLM代理根本不夠的——這是代理執行方式的結構性後果，而不是當前系統的偶然限制。共同構成安全運作的三個維度——語義意圖與政策遵循、環境有效性和動態可行性——各自依賴於在執行的不同階段可獲得的嚴格不同的信息集。沒有任何單一的護欄能夠證明這三者的合格。我們主張社群必須以基於合約的架構作出回應，其中每個安全維度由一個獨立認證的層來強制執行，其概率保證滿足下一層的假設。我們勾勒出這樣的架構，並通過概率的鏈式法則推導出它所允許的組合系統級安全界限。三個未解決的問題阻礙了這一點和可部署標準之間的距離：來自非獨立同分佈（non-i.i.d.）痕跡的界限估計、在部署漂移下合約的優雅降級，以及擴展到多代理設置——這是LLM代理運行時保證中最重要的未完成事務。
2605.18672.author: S. Bensalem et.al.
2605.18672.authors: S. Bensalem, Y. Dong, M. Franzle, X. Huang, J. Kroger, D. Nickovic, A. Nouri, R. Roy, C. Wu
